title: लक्ष्मी कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी कवचम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री लक्ष्मी कवचस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। श्री महालक्ष्मीर्देवता।
श्रीं बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। क्लीं कीलकम्।
श्री महालक्ष्मी प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
पद्मासनस्थितां देवीं पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणाम्।
पद्महस्तां पद्मवर्णां सर्वदा पद्ममालिनीम्॥
सर्वालंकारसंयुक्तां सर्वलक्षणलक्षिताम्।
सर्वदा सकलैश्वर्यसमृद्धां सर्वमंगलाम्॥

ब्रह्मोवाच —

इन्द्रादयो गुरुं नत्वा प्रोचुः पद्मभवं विभुम्।
ब्रह्मन् भगवतो लक्ष्म्याः कवचं ब्रूहि तत्त्वतः॥

ब्रह्मोवाच —

शिरो मे पातु पद्माक्षी ललाटं पातु सुन्दरी।
भ्रूमध्यं पातु कमला नेत्रे श्रीः पातु सर्वदा॥१॥

कर्णौ मे पातु वाराही नासां पातु यशस्विनी।
मुखं मे पातु चामुण्डा जिह्वां पातु सरस्वती॥२॥

दन्तान् मे पातु वैष्णवी अधरौ पातु चण्डिका।
चिबुकं पातु सौम्यास्या गलं मे कमलालया॥३॥

स्कन्धौ मे पातु जगदम्बा भुजौ मे पातु शूलिनी।
नखान् मे पातु कौमारी हृदयं पातु ईश्वरी॥४॥

स्तनौ मे पातु विश्वेशी मध्यं पातु सुरेश्वरी।
नाभिं मे पातु कामेशी कटिं मे पातु वैष्णवी॥५॥

गुह्यं मे पातु कमला ऊरू मे पातु सर्वदा।
जानुनी पातु रत्नाढ्या जंघे पातु महेश्वरी॥६॥

पादौ मे पातु सर्वज्ञा सर्वांगं पातु सर्वदा।
लक्ष्मीः सदा पातु मां देवी सर्वतः सर्वरक्षिणी॥७॥

एतत् कवचमज्ञात्वा यो जपेल्लक्ष्मीमन्त्रकम्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिः कल्पकोटिशतैरपि॥

यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय कवचं लक्ष्मिदायकम्।
सर्वान् कामानवाप्नोति लक्ष्मीवान् जायते ध्रुवम्॥

धनधान्यसमृद्धिश्च भवेत्तस्य न संशयः।
सर्वाभीष्टकरं पुंसां सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकम्॥

॥ इति ब्रह्मवैवर्तपुराणे श्री लक्ष्मी कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Kavacham ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Lakshmi Kavachasya Brahma Rishiph.
Gayatri Chhandah. Shri Mahalakshmih Devata.
Shrim Bijam. Hrim Shaktih. Klim Kilakam.
Shri Mahalakshmi Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Padmasana-sthitam Devim Padma-patra-nibhekshnam.
Padma-hastam Padma-varnam Sarvada Padma-malinim.
Sarvalamkara-samyuktam Sarva-lakshana-lakshitam.
Sarvada Sakalai-shvarya-samriddham Sarva-mangalam.

Brahma Uvacha —

Indra-adayo Gurum Natva Prochuh Padma-bhavam Vibhum.
Brahman Bhagavato Lakshmyah Kavacham Bruhi Tattvatah.

Brahma Uvacha —

Shiro Me Patu Padmakshi Lalatam Patu Sundari.
Bhru-madhyam Patu Kamala Netre Shrih Patu Sarvada.||1||

Karnau Me Patu Varahi Nasam Patu Yashasvini.
Mukham Me Patu Chamunda Jihvam Patu Sarasvati.||2||

Dantan Me Patu Vaishnavi Adharau Patu Chandika.
Chibukam Patu Saumyasya Galam Me Kamalaya.||3||

Skandhau Me Patu Jagadamba Bhujau Me Patu Shulini.
Nakhan Me Patu Kaumari Hridayam Patu Ishvari.||4||

Stanau Me Patu Vishveshi Madhyam Patu Sureshvari.
Nabhim Me Patu Kameshi Katim Me Patu Vaishnavi.||5||

Guhyam Me Patu Kamala Uru Me Patu Sarvada.
Januni Patu Ratnadhya Janghe Patu Maheshvari.||6||

Padau Me Patu Sarvajna Sarvangam Patu Sarvada.
Lakshmih Sada Patu Mam Devi Sarvatah Sarva-rakshini.||7||

Etat Kavacham-ajnatva Yo Japel-Lakshmi-mantrakam.
Na Tasya Jayate Siddhih Kalpa-koti-shatair-api.

Yah Pathet Pratar-utthaya Kavacham Lakshmi-dayakam.
Sarvan Kaman-avapnoti Lakshmivan Jayate Dhruvam.

Dhana-dhanya-samriddhish-cha Bhavet-tasya Na Samshayah.
Sarva-abhishta-karam Pumsam Sarva-sampat-pradayakam.

|| Iti Brahmavaivarta-purane Shri Lakshmi Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ब्रह्मा जी बोले — मेरे शिर की रक्षा पद्माक्षी (कमल नेत्रों वाली) करें, ललाट की सुन्दरी, भ्रूमध्य की कमला, नेत्रों की श्री (लक्ष्मी) सदा रक्षा करें। कानों की वाराही, नासिका की यशस्विनी, मुख की चामुण्डा, जिह्वा की सरस्वती रक्षा करें। दाँतों की वैष्णवी, अधरों की चण्डिका, ठोड़ी की सौम्यास्या और गले की कमलालया रक्षा करें।

कन्धों की जगदम्बा, भुजाओं की शूलिनी, नखों की कौमारी, हृदय की ईश्वरी रक्षा करें। स्तनों की विश्वेशी, मध्य भाग की सुरेश्वरी, नाभि की कामेशी, कटि की वैष्णवी रक्षा करें।

पैरों की सर्वज्ञा और सम्पूर्ण शरीर की सर्वदा लक्ष्मी देवी सब ओर से रक्षा करें। जो प्रातःकाल उठकर इस कवच का पाठ करता है, वह सब कामनाओं को प्राप्त करता है और निश्चित रूप से लक्ष्मीवान होता है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीलक्ष्मीकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता। श्रीं बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। क्लीं कीलकम्। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रीत्यर्थे लक्ष्मीकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides divine armor protection of Goddess Lakshmi over every body part
Traditionally recited to invoke permanent residence of lakshmi (wealth) in the devotee's home
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and bestows abundance of wealth and grains
Invokes all desires when recited daily in the morning
Essential prerequisite before chanting Lakshmi mantras for full efficacy
Traditionally invoked for protection from financial losses and ensures material prosperity
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Lakshmi, Lakshmi Kavacham (लक्ष्मी कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Rooted in Brahmavaivarta Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to ensure permanent residence of Lakshmi (wealth) in the devotee's home as well as provide divine armor protection of Goddess Lakshmi over every body part. Additionally, it is known to protect from financial losses and ensure material prosperity. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali and Varalakshmi Vrata. The text contains 7 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Lakshmi Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred armor hymn of Goddess Lakshmi for divine protection and lasting wealth.

seoFaqs.question: What is a kavacham and how does Lakshmi Kavacham function as one?
seoFaqs.answer: A kavacham, literally armour, is a Sanskrit hymn structured as a protective shield in which each part of the body is covered by a specific name, mantra or deity-form. Lakshmi Kavacham follows this format, asking the goddess Mahalakshmi to protect the head, eyes, ears, throat, heart, abdomen, hands, feet and the directions around the devotee. By the end of the recitation, every limb and direction has been entrusted to a specific aspect of the goddess, creating a complete circle of protection. The kavacham is therefore not only a praise hymn but a structured request for safety, prosperity and freedom from misfortune.

seoFaqs.question: From which scripture is the Lakshmi Kavacham drawn?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely circulated Mahalakshmi Kavacham is preserved in the Brahma Purana, where it is presented as composed by Lord Brahma himself for the protection of the worlds. Some shorter and parallel kavacham texts addressed to Lakshmi appear in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana and in tantric compendiums associated with Devi worship, which is why some devotees encounter the text under slightly different attributions. The core invocation, the rishi-chandas-devata structure (Brahma as rishi, Gayatri as chanda, Mahalakshmi as devata), and the body-by-body protection format are consistent across the major versions used in modern paddhatis.

seoFaqs.question: How is Lakshmi Kavacham different from Sri Suktam and Mahalakshmi Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Suktam, drawn from the Khila portion of the Rigveda, is the most ancient Vedic invocation of Lakshmi and is chanted with strict mantric pronunciation, often as part of a homa (fire ritual). Mahalakshmi Ashtakam is an eight-verse devotional praise hymn focused on the goddess's qualities. Lakshmi Kavacham is structurally a protective shield, listing body parts and asking the goddess to protect each. In a complete Lakshmi service many devotees recite all three: Sri Suktam to invoke the Vedic ground, Mahalakshmi Ashtakam to praise her qualities, and Lakshmi Kavacham to ask for her protection over body and home.

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to chant Lakshmi Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday, the day of the Devi, is the principal weekly occasion. The kavacham is also chanted on the Lakshmi Puja night of Diwali, on Dhanteras, on Akshaya Tritiya, on Varalakshmi Vratam in the month of Shravan, and on every full-moon night that falls under a Lakshmi-related nakshatra. Many households chant it at dawn after lighting a ghee lamp and offering kumkum, lotus or red flowers. Periods of personal financial difficulty, prolonged illness in the family, or anxiety about home protection are also considered suitable times to take a forty-one-day sankalpa of daily recitation.

seoFaqs.question: Who is eligible to chant Lakshmi Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: As a Puranic stotra rather than a Vedic mantra, Lakshmi Kavacham is widely treated as open to any devotee, regardless of caste, gender or formal initiation. Householders, women, children and seekers of every background recite it at home shrines and community Lakshmi pujas. Some traditions emphasise that strict kavacham use, where each body-part nyasa is performed with the corresponding mantra, benefits from a teacher's guidance. Simple recitation as a praise and protection prayer, however, requires only cleanliness, a clear intention, and respectful posture facing the deity image. Many devotees first read the meaning in their own language so that the protective intent is clear before chanting in Sanskrit.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do practitioners traditionally associate with Lakshmi Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacham itself promises protection from poverty, disease, untimely death, hostile influences, debt, and from negative planetary periods that disturb household stability. Beyond these protective fruits, devotees describe a steadying psychological effect: the act of mentally placing the goddess at every part of the body cultivates a felt sense of being held, which reduces financial anxiety even before circumstances change. Hindu wealth ethics consistently link Lakshmi's continued presence to dharmic behaviour at home, so the kavacham is treated as a complement to honest livelihood, regular charity, respect for elders and cleanliness rather than as a stand-alone guarantee of prosperity.
